description: Gravitational pull of the planet causes the star to wobble, detected via Doppler shift.
Planet directly imaged using advanced telescope techniques.
Variations in eclipse timing in binary star systems indicate a third body (planet).
Planet's gravity bends and amplifies light from a background star.
Planet passes in front of its star, causing a measurable dip in brightness.
Variations in the timing of transits caused by gravitational interaction with other planets.
Tiny positional shift of the star caused by an orbiting planet is measured.
Planet detected through its effect on the motion of material in a protoplanetary disk.
Detects planets by measuring periodic brightness changes of the star caused by the orbiting planet reflecting light or causing tidal distortions.
Pulsating star timing is altered by a companion planet.
Regular pulsar signals are disrupted by the gravitational influence of a planet.
